_description: Risk factor rules for probability adjustments. Each factor adjusts the base approval rate up or down based on clinical documentation and payer behavior.

factors.name: Physical therapy records present
factors.description: PT records document conservative treatment failure, which most payers require before approving advanced procedures.

payer_notes.United Healthcare: United requires 6+ weeks PT documentation for lumbar spine imaging
payer_notes.Aetna: Aetna requires documented PT with objective measures of progress

factors.name: Missing prior imaging report
factors.description: Payer requires prior imaging before approving this procedure, but no imaging results found in documentation.

payer_notes.United Healthcare: United requires MRI within 6 months for surgical procedures
payer_notes.Cigna: Cigna requires imaging confirming structural pathology

factors.name: Diagnosis matches payer criteria
factors.description: Primary ICD-10 diagnosis directly matches the payer's approved indications for this procedure.

factors.name: Prior denial for same CPT code
factors.description: Patient has a prior denial for the same procedure code, which statistically correlates with higher re-denial rates unless new evidence is presented.

factors.name: High-scrutiny payer for this code
factors.description: This payer has significantly higher denial rates for this procedure category compared to market average.

payer_notes.United Healthcare: United has the highest commercial denial rate for lumbar spine and pain management codes
payer_notes.Aetna: Aetna applies strict step-therapy requirements for specialty pharmacy

factors.name: Conservative treatment failure documented
factors.description: Documentation shows patient failed conservative treatments (medications, PT, injections) before requesting this procedure.

payer_notes.United Healthcare: United requires documented failure of 3+ months conservative care
payer_notes.Aetna: Aetna requires step-therapy documentation with objective outcomes

factors.name: Adequate symptom duration documented
factors.description: Symptoms have persisted long enough to meet payer duration requirements (typically 6+ weeks for imaging, 3+ months for surgery).

factors.name: Functional impairment documented
factors.description: Documentation includes specific functional limitations (ADL restrictions, work impact, mobility impairment).

factors.name: BMI documented for obesity-related procedures
factors.description: BMI >= 30 documented for weight-related medication or procedure, meeting medical necessity threshold.

payer_notes.United Healthcare: United requires BMI >= 30 (or >= 27 with comorbidity) for semaglutide
payer_notes.Aetna: Aetna requires BMI >= 30 and documented diet/exercise failure for 6+ months

factors.name: Missing required lab results
factors.description: Required laboratory results not found in clinical documentation.

payer_notes.United Healthcare: United requires HbA1c and metabolic panel for semaglutide PA

factors.name: Medication trial documented
factors.description: Documentation shows patient tried and failed relevant medications before this procedure or specialty drug.

factors.name: Specialist referral documented
factors.description: Referral from PCP or specialist supports the medical necessity of the requested procedure.

factors.name: Incomplete clinical documentation
factors.description: Clinical notes are missing key sections (history, exam findings, or assessment) that payers require for medical necessity determination.

factors.name: Peer-reviewed evidence cited
factors.description: Clinical documentation or justification letter cites peer-reviewed clinical guidelines supporting the procedure.
